nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Department Permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department Permission. Departmental permission only; Instructor will arrange meeting with students

nodes.title: Microbiol & Infectious Disease
nodes.description: An introduction to basic microbiology and microbes that cause infectious diseases, with a focus on microbial structure, function, metabolism, ecology, and pathogenesis. Prerequisites: BHSC 1340, BCOR 1400, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1005 or BIOL 1400; Microbiology or Molecular Genetics major or minor or Animal Sciences, Biomedical and Clinical Sciences major.
nodes.other_requirements: Microbiology or Molecular Genetics major or minor or Animal Sciences, Biomedical and Clinical Sciences major. Minimum sophomore standing; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE and Post Bac Pre Med students register for MMG 2010 ZRA; Eligible majors: ASCI, BISC, ENSC, MGEN, MICR, MLS, PBIO, HSCI, PHS,BMCS majors and MICR minors only until 5/01/2026.

nodes.title: Applied Molecular Biology
nodes.description: Introduces students to the nucleic acid and protein-based molecular diagnostics technology through class presentation, reading, and discussions. Focuses on diagnostic applications for understanding molecular mechanisms of disease. Credit not awarded for both BHSC 3810 and BHSC 5810. Prerequisite: CHEM 1580 or CHEM 2580.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS/BMCS students must take lecture and lab (BHSC 3820); Med. diag. minor students need lecture only; Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Intro Organic Chemistry w/lab
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of basic organic compounds of technological and biological significance. Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1152, CHEM 1582, CHEM 1500, or CHEM 2580. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and  CHEM 1150 ,  CHEM 1152 ,  CHEM 1582 ,  CHEM 1500 , or  CHEM 2580 . Must register for a CHEM 1580 lab, L01-L15; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies; Cross listed withCHEM 1582A. PACE students with permission and override. All labs must be completed in order to pass the course. Attendance for all lectures and exams is expected. All work must be completed by the due date. The final exam must be taken to pass the course.

nodes.title: Spec Top: Hum Cell Bio for Nur
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. Contact Instructor for more information if needed.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Individual research performed under the supervision of a faculty mentor. A written report and seminar is required. Prerequisite: Department Permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department Permission. Instructor Permission required. Contact Instructor for more info if needed

nodes.title: Applied Molecular Biology Lab
nodes.description: Laboratory experiences include practical concepts of molecular applications. Introduces basic methods used in DNA and Protein technology including plasmid isolation, polymerase chain reaction, restriction enzyme use, and related assays. Credit not awarded for both BHSC 3820 and BHSC 5820. Prerequisite: CHEM 1580 or CHEM 2580. Co-requisite: BHSC 3810.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS, BMCS or MLS PBC PACE students only; Molecular Bio Lab Fee: $70.00; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Pre-Calculus Mathematics
nodes.description: Skills in working with numerical, algebraic, and trigonometric expressions are developed in preparation for MATH 1234. May not be taken for credit concurrently with, or following receipt of, credit for any mathematics course numbered MATH 1234 or above. Prerequisite: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 1
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of organic compounds with consideration of bonding, stereochemistry, and reaction mechanisms. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2580 and CHEM 1580, CHEM 1582, or CHEM 1500. Prerequisite: CHEM 1450 or CHEM 1455.
nodes.other_requirements: $85 fee for lab supplies; Must register for a CHEM 2580 lab L01-L23; Degree students only; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2580 ZRA-ZRC

nodes.title: Immunology
nodes.description: Deals with cells, organs, development, interactions and the functioning (infectious process, immunodeficiency, hypersensitivity reactions, transplantation and tumor immunology) of the innate and the adaptive immune system. Prerequisites: One semester of cell biology or similar course is encouraged.
nodes.other_requirements: One semester of cell biology or similar course is encouraged. Degree students only, PACE students register for BHSC 3420 ZRA

nodes.title: Written Expression
nodes.description: A foundational composition course featuring a sequence of writing, reading, and information literacy assignments. Students learn to write and revise for different rhetorical situations while increasing their mastery of academic conventions. Some sections designed for specific student audiences.

nodes.title: Intro to Scientific Writing
nodes.description: Introduction to the principles and practices of research and writing in the biomedical and health sciences. Using scientific data and literature as a foundation, students will write in multiple genres through regular assignments applicable to future course work and health science professions. Pre/co-requisites: ENGL 1001 or equivalent; Radiation Medical Science, Medical Laboratory Sciences, Health Sciences major; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Radiation Medical Science, Medical Laboratory Sciences, Health Sciences major; or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; TA for HSCI Courses

nodes.title: Immunology Lab
nodes.description: Laboratory experience dealing with cellular and humoral immunity, B cells and T cells, autoimmunity, immunodeficiency. Laboratory covers immunological techniques and applications. Credit not awarded for both BHSC 3440 and BHSC 5440. Prerequisites: One semester of biochemistry, one semester of organic chemistry. Co-requisites: BHSC 3420 or MMG 3230.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS majors or Instructor permission: Lab fee: $65; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to calculus of functions of one variable including: limits, continuity, techniques and applications of differentiation and integration. Credit not given for more than one course in the pair MATH 1212, MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 1248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system.

nodes.title: Radiation Science
nodes.description: Provides a broad based understanding of the fundamentals of radiation science including the ways in which radiation is produced and utilized, the principles of radioactive decay, radiation exposure, absorbed dose, shielding and detection of radiation. Prerequisite: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234. Co-requisites: RADT 2520 or Instructor permission for non-majors.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisites: RADT 2520 or Instructor permission for non-majors. Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: College Algebra
nodes.description: Sets, relations, functions with particular attention to properties of algebraic, exponential, logarithmic functions, their graphs and applications in preparation for MATH 1212. May not be taken for credit concurrently with, or following receipt of, credit for any mathematics course numbered MATH 1212 or above. Pre/co-requisites: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to limits and differential/integral calculus with a wide variety of applications. Students interested in intensive use of mathematics should take MATH 1234. Credit not awarded for both MATH 1212 and MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248. See MATH 1242. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system: MATH 1012 or MATH 1034 or ALEKS MPA of at least 61; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Advanced Radiation Science
nodes.description: Lecture and laboratory experiences to enhance the understanding and application of the principles of radioactive decay, radiation exposure, absorbed dose, shielding and detection of radiation. Prerequisite: MATH 1012, MATH 1034, MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 2
nodes.description: Part II of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body. Prerequisite: ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: Common hour exams are held 5:30-7:00pm on these Mondays: 2/9, 3/2, and 4/6. BIOM,CSD,DIET,DNFS,EXSC,HSCI,MLS,NFS, PE, PHS, MRS, PRNU; Majors only until Dec. 2nd, Please contact Patrick Mullen (patrick.c.mullen@uvm.edu) with any questions; Class meets 3x a week with online lab comprising 4th credit; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for ANPS 1200 ZRA; Prereq enforced by the system:ANPS 1190.

nodes.title: Leadership & Mgt in Hlth Care
nodes.description: Familiarizes students with operational aspects of health care management, leadership and policy. Explores current techniques in process improvement, management methodologies, and healthcare policy with a special focus on disparities in health and healthcare. Prerequisites: College of Nursing and Health Sciences majors; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: College of Nursing and Health Sciences majors; minimum Junior standing. Must be 3rd year standing.

nodes.title: Cross Sectional Imaging
nodes.description: Introduction to the radiographic anatomy and the various imaging modalities presently used to include diagnostic imaging, computed tomography (CT), magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), and nuclear medicine. Prerequisites: ANPS 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: other degree students with instructor permission

nodes.title: Human Cell Biology
nodes.description: Lecture and laboratory experiences about molecular and cellular structure, function and physiology using human cells as the model.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for Lab A01, A02, or A03; students only or Instructor permission; Student Lab Fee: $65.00; Degree students only, PACE students register for BHSC 1340 ZRA
